'''Sam Eseh''' (born 30 June 2003) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Betfred Super League]].

He has played for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]]. He spent time on loan from [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] at the [[Barrow Raiders]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. Eseh has spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Leeds Rhinos]] and two separate spells at [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League|top flight]].<ref>[https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/sam-eseh-52429.html itsrugby profile]</ref>

==Background==
Eseh was born in [[Leeds, West Yorkshire]], England and raised in [[Halton Moor]], Leeds.<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-fc-star-sam-eseh-lifts-lid-on-mothers-cancer-journey-in-emotional-interview Hull FC star Sam Eseh lifts lid on mother’s cancer journey in emotional interview]</ref> He played for [[East Leeds ARLFC|East Leeds]] and the [[Chapeltown Cougars]] as a junior.<ref>[https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2023/10/31/getting-to-know-sam-eseh-jr/ Get to know our latest signing Sam Eseh Jr who arrived on a two-year deal from Wakefield Trinity earlier this month.]</ref>

He joined the Wakefield Trinity Scholarship and Academy system, and graduated into the [[Wakefield Trinity|Trinity]] first team squad ahead of the 2021 season.<ref>[https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/3819/eseh-joins-the-champions Eseh Joins the Champions]</ref>

==Career==
Eseh made his professional debut in April 2022 on loan from Wakefield Trinity at the [[Barrow Raiders]] against the [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>[https://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/halifax-panthers-40-barrow-raiders-18-fourth-win-on-spin-for-panthers-3666647 Halifax Panthers 40 Barrow Raiders 18: Fourth win on spin for Panthers]</ref>

In June 2022 he was sent on loan to [[Featherstone Rovers]] for the remainder of the [[2022 RFL Championship]] season.<ref>[https://wakefieldtrinity.com/eseh-joins-featherstone-on-loan-deal/ Eseh joins Featherstone on loan deal]</ref>

Eseh made his [[Super League]] debut for Wakefield Trinity against the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in March 2023.<ref>[https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/star-of-the-future-wakefield-trinity-youngster-sam-eseh-makes-mark-in-super-league-4069253 Star of the future: Wakefield Trinity youngster Sam Eseh makes mark in Super League]</ref>

He joined the Wigan Warriors on a two-year deal, with the option of a third, following the conclusion of his Wakefield Trinity deal at the end of the [[2023 Super League season]].<ref>[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67200164 Sam Eseh: Wigan Warriors sign Wakefield Trinity forward on two-year deal]</ref>

Eseh spent April and May 2024 on loan from the [[Wigan Warriors|Warriors]], over two separate spells at the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>[https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2024/05/08/sam-eseh-rejoins-castleford-on-loan/ Sam Eseh rejoins Castleford on loan]</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=23 April 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers continue recruitment drive with signing of former Wakefield Trinity prop Sam Eseh |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-continue-recruitment-drive-with-signing-of-former-wakefield-trinity-prop-sam-eseh-4602246 |access-date=23 April 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=1 May 2024 |title=Sam Eseh returns to Wigan Warriors as Matt Peet provides update on prop |url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/sam-eseh-returns-to-wigan-warriors-as-matt-peet-provides-update-on-prop-4612119 |access-date=3 May 2024 |website=Wigan Today}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=7 May 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers secure loan return of Wigan Warriors forward after injury |url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-loan-wigan-warriors-29123547 |access-date=8 May 2024 |website=Yorkshire Live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Bower |first=Aaron |date=22 May 2024 |title=Tiaki Chan update as Hull FC loan situation explained by Wigan Warriors coach |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/tiaki-chan-update-as-hull-fc-loan-situation-explained-by-wigan-warriors-coach |access-date=22 May 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>

He spent June and July 2024 on loan from Wigan at the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>[https://www.therhinos.co.uk/teams/profile/1057/samuel-eseh 36 Samuel Eseh]</ref><ref>[https://www.totalrl.com/matt-peet-explains-reasoning-behind-sam-eseh-recall-if-jai-field-will-play-and-bevan-french-latest-for-wigan-warriors/ Matt Peet explains reasoning behind Sam Eseh recall, if Jai Field will play and Bevan French latest for Wigan Warriors]</ref>

Following a short recall to his parent club, Eseh was sent on loan to [[Hull FC]] in August 2024 for the remainder of the season.<ref>[https://www.hullfc.com/blog/2024/08/05/eseh-joins-on-loan/ Eseh Joins On Loan]</ref>

He spent the 2025 season on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite news |last1=Olawumi |first1=Ben |title=Wigan Warriors forward makes Super League switch for remainder of 2025 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-warriors-forward-makes-super-league-switch-for-remainder-of-2025 |access-date=27 February 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=26 February 2025}}</ref>
